# ADR-004: Two Separate NuGet Packages

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The SDK serves two distinct consumer profiles:

1. Pure .NET applications (console, worker services, Blazor, etc.) that only need Contentful API access — they should not need to pull in ASP.NET Core dependencies.
2. ASP.NET Core web applications that also want Tag Helpers, Middleware, and DI integration.

Bundling ASP.NET Core dependencies into the core package would force all consumers to accept heavy ASP.NET runtime dependencies they may not need or want.

## Decision

Ship two NuGet packages:
- **`contentful.csharp`** — `Contentful.Core` project. No ASP.NET Core dependencies. All Contentful API clients and models live here.
- **`contentful.aspnetcore`** — `Contentful.AspNetCore` project. Depends on `contentful.csharp` + ASP.NET Core packages. Provides DI registration, Tag Helpers, Middleware, and Authoring utilities.

Both packages are versioned in lockstep (same `VersionPrefix`) and published manually to NuGet.

## Consequences

- **Enables:** Non-ASP.NET consumers install only `contentful.csharp` with a minimal dependency footprint.
- **Requires:** Manual versioning and publishing of both packages in every release. The AspNetCore package must always reference the same version of the core package. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](../../CONTRIBUTING.md) for the release procedure.
- **Invariant:** Never create a circular dependency between the two packages.
